task:
Atom 0 of the 'Harden the documentation process' priority: establish the DOC-TYPE TAXONOMY and its declaration convention as the decision layer the later CI reference-check will act on. Do NOT build the check script yet (next atom) and do NOT reclassify every doc yet — this atom is the taxonomy definition, the front-matter convention, and the governed-doc-set boundary, in ONE owner doc.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Make docs/freshness-policy.md the single owner of the documentation-truth process (it already owns freshness stamps; extend it, do NOT create a parallel taxonomy doc — shared Elegance Standard: one owner per concept). Add a 'Doc-type taxonomy' section that defines the four founder-approved types from the priority Objective: current-truth, design-intent, owner-map, historical. For EACH type give: (a) a one-line definition, (b) the reference/freshness rule the future CI check will apply — i.e. whether a doc of this type MUST have its concrete references (internal markdown links, file/dir paths, ADR ids, CLI commands/flags, package names, named code symbols) resolve against the live tree, or is exempt because it is aspirational (design-intent) or a record (historical), and WHY.
2. Define the front-matter declaration convention: a governed doc declares its type with a `doc-type: <one of current-truth|design-intent|owner-map|historical>` front-matter field, alongside the existing Status / Last verified stamps. State explicitly the DEFAULT when the field is absent: treat as current-truth (strictest), so a new unclassified doc is checked strictly rather than silently skipped.
3. Define the 'governed doc set' the process covers, as concrete globs (e.g. docs/**/*.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, cocoder/decisions/*.md, cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md, packages/personas/base/**/*.md). List them in the owner doc so the next atom's check has a single boundary to read. Flag in prose any governed doc currently outside a committable write-lane (root README.md/CONTRIBUTING.md) as a known gap to resolve in a later atom — do not try to fix scopes here.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- docs/freshness-policy.md contains a 'Doc-type taxonomy' section covering all four types, each with its definition AND its per-type reference/freshness rule (strict-resolve vs exempt, with reason).
- The `doc-type` front-matter field name and its four allowed values are specified, plus the default-when-absent = current-truth rule.
- The governed-doc-set globs are enumerated in the owner doc.
- No second/parallel taxonomy doc is created; existing freshness-policy.md content is preserved (don't delete the stamp/cadence sections).

MUST NOT BREAK: keep one owner (no parallel contract). If any existing test pins freshness-policy.md content, update it consistently. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test` and report exit codes; if a doc-content test exists for freshness-policy.md, it must stay green. Write scope: docs/** only for this atom.

Atom 1 of 'Harden the documentation process': build the DOC-REFERENCE RESOLVER as a library function and PROVE it detects a planted broken reference — by EXTENDING the existing drift engine, not building a parallel extractor. Do NOT wire it as a real-tree CI gate yet (that is atom 2). Stay library + unit tests this atom.

CONTEXT / REUSE (mandatory — one-owner, shared Elegance Standard): the repo already has a reference engine at packages/core/src/drift/ — readGovernanceClaims (read-claims.ts) extracts references {kind:'path'|'glob'} with {file,line} evidence; readRepoReality (read-reality.ts) snapshots the live tree; compareDrift (compare.ts) emits findings 'stale-path-reference'|'dead-scope-glob'; runDriftAudit/buildDriftReport produce the advisory report consumed by scripts/run-drift-audit.mjs. You MUST build on these modules and their reference/finding types. Do NOT create a second reference extractor, a second reality reader, or a parallel finding type. The existing advisory drift-audit path and its tests (packages/core/tests/drift-read-claims.test.ts and any other drift tests) MUST stay green.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add a resolver entrypoint in packages/core/src/drift/ (e.g. resolveDocReferences) that, given a repoRoot, reads the GOVERNED DOC SET defined in docs/freshness-policy.md (docs/**/*.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, cocoder/decisions/*.md, cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md, packages/personas/base/**/*.md), parses each doc's `doc-type` front-matter (default current-truth when absent), and returns the list of UNRESOLVED concrete references with {file,line,kind,value,reason}. Apply the taxonomy strictness from freshness-policy.md: resolve strictly for current-truth and owner-map; SKIP design-intent and historical (exempt). The governed-glob list should have ONE executable owner — define it once in the drift module and have freshness-policy.md's prose point to that as the executable source (avoid a doc/code copy that can itself drift).
2. Reference categories to resolve — implement the low-false-positive set reliably, reusing the existing path/glob resolution where it already works: (a) internal markdown links — relative link targets `[..](path)` must exist on disk; (b) file/dir paths mentioned in backticks; (c) ADR ids — `ADR-NNNN` must map to an existing cocoder/decisions/NNNN-*.md; (d) package names — `@cocoder/<pkg>` must have a packages/<pkg>. ALSO implement (e) CLI commands/flags — `cocoder ...` invocations and `--flags` resolved against the real CLI surface in packages/cli/src/run.ts — ONLY if you can do it with low false positives; if not clean, leave a clearly-named TODO/follow-up note in the resolver and report it rather than shipping a noisy check. Treat named-code-symbol resolution as OUT of this atom (high false-positive); note it as a deferred follow-up. Trustworthiness over coverage: a gate that cries wolf is worse than a narrower reliable one.
3. Unit tests (packages/core/tests/, fixture pattern from drift-read-claims.test.ts using mkdtemp + writeFixtureFile): prove the resolver (i) RESOLVES a doc whose references all exist (returns empty), (ii) FLAGS a planted broken reference of each implemented category (returns the unresolved ref with correct file:line), and (iii) EXEMPTS a design-intent and a historical doc with a broken reference (returns empty for those). This planted-broken-reference test is the priority's required proof that the mechanism turns red — it runs under `pnpm test`, so it is in CI. Do NOT leave any broken reference in the REAL tree; all planting happens in tmpdir fixtures.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A resolver function exists in packages/core/src/drift/ (exported via index.ts), reuses readRepoReality/compare semantics rather than re-implementing tree reads, honors doc-type strictness, and returns unresolved refs with file:line evidence.
- New unit tests prove: clean tree → no findings; a planted broken ref of each implemented category → a finding at the right file:line; design-intent/historical docs with broken refs → no findings.
- The governed-glob list has a single executable owner referenced by the doc.
- Existing drift tests and the advisory drift-audit path remain unchanged in behavior and green.

MUST NOT BREAK: don't fork a parallel extractor/reality-reader/finding-type; don't change the advisory report output; don't touch .github (out of scope) — CI inclusion comes free via `pnpm test` running the new vitest tests. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test`; report exit codes and the new test names. Write scope: packages/** and docs/** (docs only to update the freshness-policy.md prose pointer to the executable glob owner).

Atom 2 of 'Harden the documentation process': turn the atom-1 resolver into a REAL-TREE CI GATE that runs under `pnpm test` and fails on NEW unresolved references, shipping GREEN on the current tree via a small precise baseline. This is the priority's load-bearing deliverable.

CRITICAL CONTEXT (Oscar ran resolveDocReferences over the live repo): it returns 1774 findings, but they are dominated by STRUCTURAL FALSE POSITIVES, not real drift: 1750 are code-span `path` mentions that are placeholders/conceptual/command strings in governance docs (e.g. `directive-<n>.json`, `pickup.md`, `playbook/P1/intent.json`, `node scripts/check-topology.mjs`) — not committed files; 22 are `adr` references to ADR-0015/0021/0022 which are INTENTIONALLY RETIRED (ADR-0034 retires 0015) — legitimate historical references; 2 are `markdown-link` (a `NNNN-slug.md` template placeholder and a link in a historical audit doc). A gate that fires on all of these would bury real drift in noise. Gate only the HIGH-CONFIDENCE kinds with a tiny baseline.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add a CI-gating vitest test in packages/core/tests/ (it runs under the existing `pnpm test` CI seam — do NOT touch .github, which is out of scope) that calls resolveDocReferences over the REAL repoRoot, FILTERS to the gated kinds only — `markdown-link`, `adr`, `package` — subtracts a committed baseline allowlist, and FAILS (expect empty) on any remaining finding. The failure message must print file:line:kind:value:reason for each new finding so a future red is actionable.
2. Code-span `path` kind is NOT gated this atom — it needs a precision filter (exclude placeholder tokens `<>`/`NNNN`/`*`, command strings with spaces, and bare paths without a known repo root) before it is trustworthy. Add it to the existing deferredDocReferenceChecks list with that reason. Keep the resolver still EMITTING path findings (advisory/unchanged); only the gate excludes them.
3. Add the baseline as a small committed allowlist owned in the drift module (e.g. packages/core/src/drift/doc-references-baseline.ts, exported), expressed by (kind, value) — NOT by file:line — so it does not re-introduce brittle line anchors (problem-inventory item 2). Seed it from the current tree: the retired ADR ids (`ADR-0015`, `ADR-0021`, `ADR-0022`) and the two tolerated markdown-link values. Each baseline entry carries a one-line reason. The gate treats a finding as tolerated when its (kind,value) is in the baseline.
4. Prove the GATE turns red on NEW drift (distinct from atom 1's resolver-level proof): a unit test that feeds the gate's compare step a findings list containing one entry NOT in the baseline and asserts the gate reports it as a failure (non-empty). Pure function over inputs — no real-tree planting.
5. Update docs/freshness-policy.md (single owner): document that the reference gate runs in CI via `pnpm test`; which kinds are gated (markdown-link/adr/package) vs deferred (code-span path precision filter, CLI commands/flags, named code symbols); and that the baseline is the tolerated pre-existing snapshot, worked down by the separate Doc Truth Analysis priority — this priority builds the guardrail, not the content re-audit.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `pnpm test` is GREEN on the current tree with the new real-tree gate active (the gate sees zero un-baselined high-confidence findings).
- The gate fails loudly if a NEW markdown-link/package/ADR reference does not resolve and is not baselined (proven by the unit test in step 4).
- Baseline is (kind,value)-based, small, each entry reasoned; no file:line anchors.
- Code-span path / CLI / symbol gating are recorded as deferred with reasons; resolver advisory output unchanged; atom-1 tests and existing drift tests stay green.

MUST NOT BREAK: no parallel resolver; don't touch .github; don't weaken the resolver to hide findings (the gate filters by kind/baseline, the resolver still reports all). If you find a gated finding that is genuinely real drift fixable inside docs/** or packages/** (Bob's write scope), fix it instead of baselining it, and say so. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test`; report exit codes, the gate test name, and the final baseline entries. Write scope: packages/** and docs/**.

Atom 3 of 'Harden the documentation process': establish the WORKLIST-ARCHIVE CONVENTION and apply it as the seed migration to the two existing audit worklists. Both files already declare (in their Status blockquote) that their archival is owned by this priority — so this atom executes that.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Create the worklist-archive home `docs/archive/` and move BOTH files there together: `docs/architecture-truth-audit.md` -> `docs/archive/architecture-truth-audit.md`, and `docs/docs-files-truth-audit.md` -> `docs/archive/docs-files-truth-audit.md`. The old `docs/<file>.md` paths must no longer exist.
2. Add `doc-type: historical` front matter to each moved file (so the reference gate treats them as exempt evidence records). Preserve their existing body/Status content; you may update the Status blockquote to note the archival is now done under this priority.
3. Fix the relative links INSIDE the moved files so they still resolve from the new `docs/archive/` depth: their Status blockquotes link to `./phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md` (now one level up -> `../phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md`) and the two files reference each other. Update those intra-doc relative links to the correct new paths.
4. Document the worklist-archive convention in the SINGLE owner doc `docs/freshness-policy.md` (do NOT create a parallel convention doc): one-shot audit/reconciliation worklists are moved to `docs/archive/` and set `doc-type: historical` when their owning priority completes, so audit artifacts do not accumulate permanently in `docs/`. Keep it tight — a short subsection.
5. The doc-reference baseline entry in `packages/core/src/drift/doc-references-baseline.ts` for the markdown-link value `cocoder/tickets/open/0037-contributing-pr-template-stale-rg-ci-gate.md` came from `architecture-truth-audit.md`; once that file is `historical` (exempt) the resolver no longer emits it, so that baseline entry is now DEAD — remove it (elegance: shrink the baseline). Leave the `NNNN-slug.md` placeholder entry. Confirm the gate stays green after removal.

DO NOT TOUCH (out of scope or wrong to rewrite): `cocoder/SESSION_LOG.md` (append-only historical log, Oscar scope — its links to the old paths are an acceptable dated record and not in the governed gate set); the priority spec `cocoder/priorities/harden-documentation-process.md`; and the archived priority `cocoder/priorities/archive/doc-truth-analysis.md`. Backtick code-span mentions of the old paths in `docs/phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md` are ungated `path` kind; you MAY update them to the new `docs/archive/` path for accuracy, but do not let that expand the diff if it gets noisy.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Both worklists live at `docs/archive/` with `doc-type: historical`; old paths gone.
- Intra-doc relative links in the moved files resolve from the new location.
- The archive convention is documented once in `docs/freshness-policy.md`.
- The dead baseline markdown-link entry is removed; `docs/archive/**/*.md` is matched by the governed glob `docs/**/*.md` but exempt via `historical`, so the gate does NOT flag the moved files.
- `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test` are green (the doc-reference gate test in particular stays green with zero un-baselined findings).

MUST NOT BREAK: move both files together (a half-move would strand cross-links); don't reintroduce a parallel convention doc; don't weaken the gate. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test`; report exit codes and confirm the two files' new paths and the baseline now has exactly one entry. Write scope: docs/** and packages/**.

Atom 4 of 'Harden the documentation process': APPLY the doc-type taxonomy across the governed doc corpus by adding explicit `doc-type` front matter to the docs that are NOT current-truth. Oscar has done the classification judgment; you apply it. Per the elegance standard, do NOT stamp `doc-type: current-truth` on every file — current-truth is the documented default, so only docs whose correct type differs from the default get an explicit marker. This makes the taxonomy real and (for the exempt types) prevents the gate from future-flagging aspirational/historical docs.

ADD `doc-type` FRONT MATTER (insert a `---\ndoc-type: <type>\n---\n` block as the very first lines, preserving all existing content) to EXACTLY these files:

design-intent (aspirational / does-not-change-runtime):
- docs/oz-design-brief.md  (Status: intent — pending design output)
- docs/oz-streaming-design.md  (read-only design artifact; does not change runtime behavior)

owner-map (ownership/consumer maps; stay strict-resolve — marker is for type clarity + freshness discipline):
- docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md
- docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md
- docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md

historical (completed dated record / superseded analysis / diagnosis-of-record — these reference deleted/old surfaces and must be EXEMPT from strict resolution):
- docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md  ("Completed historical analysis"; references deleted executor code)
- docs/loop-packets-retrofit-audit.md  (completed audit with founder verdict)
- docs/founder-brief-format-durability.md  (diagnosis of record for a completed repair, not a proposal)

DO NOT add markers to any other governed doc. All remaining governed docs (the rest of docs/**, ARCHITECTURE.md, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, cocoder/decisions/*.md, cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md, packages/personas/base/**) are correctly current-truth by default and need no front matter. Do NOT touch files outside docs/** (cocoder/** and root README/CONTRIBUTING are out of your write scope; they need no edit anyway).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The 8 files above each begin with the correct `doc-type` front matter; existing content preserved (no body edits).
- No other doc receives a doc-type marker.
- The doc-reference gate stays GREEN: the 5 design-intent/historical files become exempt (resolver emits nothing for them), the 3 owner-maps stay strict (must still produce zero un-baselined gated findings). If marking an owner-map strict surfaces a NEW gated finding (broken markdown-link/package/ADR), report it — do not baseline it blindly; it may be a real fix.

MUST NOT BREAK: front matter must parse (valid `---` fenced block); don't reorder or edit doc bodies; don't add a parallel classification registry (front matter is the one declaration mechanism). Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test`; report exit codes and confirm the gate test passes. Write scope: docs/** only.
